mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of rising rate periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive INDPRO growth during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in consumer price inflation reduces expectations for aggressive central bank tightening, lowering discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain negative in >60% of low-inflation months despite the threshold crossing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with >55% accuracy over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms expanding economic output and corporate revenue streams, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% month-over-month signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of periods following a -0.5% INDPRO decline.
